findings.title: pattern-scan.sh unquoted variables cause word-splitting
findings.why_it_matters: When project paths or Java filenames contain spaces, shell word-splitting breaks grep file-list arguments. Filenames starting with '-' may be interpreted as grep options (option injection), altering scan behavior or causing errors.
findings.suggested_fix: Quote all file-list variables passed to grep (e.g. "$controllers" → use arrays or xargs -0). Add '--' before filenames, e.g.: grep -h '^@RestController' -- $controllers
evidence: scripts/pattern-scan.sh:257-292: multiple grep commands using unquoted $entities
scripts/pattern-scan.sh:333-349: multiple grep commands using unquoted $all_configs

residual_risks: scripts/check-gate-2-exit.sh reads COMPLIANCE_FILE and SPEC_FILE from user-provided PROJECT_DIR; path traversal could read arbitrary files, but the script runs with the invoking user's privileges so impact is limited.
Integration tests create predictable /tmp directories (tinypowers-test-*-$$); symlink race conditions are theoretically possible but low impact in a test context.
testing_gaps: No integration tests exercise pattern-scan.sh with filenames containing spaces, glob characters, or leading dashes.
No tests verify that gate scripts reject or safely handle symlinked or traversal-containing PROJECT_DIR arguments.
